message:
So last week my kid asked me, 'Mom, where's the flashlight?' and I said, 'It's in the emergency kit,' confidently. Spoiler: it wasn't. What *was* in there was a dead battery, two old granola bars I'd forgotten about, and a note from 2019 that just said 'check this.' 😅

I spent 20 minutes laughing at myself while actually *building* a real go-bag this time. And honestly? The funny part wasn't the chaos—it was realizing how much lighter I felt once I actually *knew* where things were.

Preparedness doesn't have to be this intense, serious thing. Sometimes it's just... keeping your promises to yourself. This little refresher helped me stop overthinking it.

What's the one thing you keep *meaning* to sort out but haven't yet?
